objectID: 2026/2026-01-13 - The Languages of Africa/2026-01-13 - The Languages of Africa.html#map
href: 2026/2026-01-13 - The Languages of Africa/2026-01-13 - The Languages of Africa.html#map
title: The Languages of Africa
text:
Map

n_lang <- africa |> 
  summarise(n = n(),
            .by = country)

africa_map <- ne_countries(continent = "Africa",
                       scale = "medium",
                       returnclass = "sf") |> 
    mutate(admin = recode(admin,
    "United Republic of Tanzania" = "Tanzania",
    "eSwatini"   = "Eswatini"
  )) |> 

  left_join(n_lang, by = c("admin" = "country"))

plot <- ggplot(africa_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = n), color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    option = "viridis",
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20),
    na.value = "grey60",
    name = "Number of \nAfrican Languages"
  ) +
  coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 25) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "inside",
    legend.position.inside = c(0.25, 0.35),
    legend.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.75, "cm"),
    legend.key.height = unit(2, "cm"),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Cameroon has the highest linguistic diversity \nfor natively spoken languages in Africa"
  ) +
  add_caption_cwb(type = "plot", include_data_source = TRUE,
           data_source = "Robert Muwanga & Wikipedia")

# Save and display images
current_dir <- dirname(knitr::current_input())

ggsave(plot = plot, 
       dpi = "screen",
       width = 17,
       height = 20,
       device = ragg::agg_png,
       filename = file.path(current_dir, "african_languages.png"))


# Read the big plot
img <- image_read(file.path(current_dir, "african_languages.png"))

# Force 16:9 aspect ratio with minimal padding
# Target size: 1200x675 px (16:9)
img_card <- image_scale(img, "1200x675")           # scale to fit inside 16:9
img_card <- image_extent(
  img_card,
  geometry = "1200x675",
  gravity = "center"
)

# Save as card preview
image_write(img_card, path = file.path(current_dir, "preview.png"))

knitr::include_graphics(
  file.path(current_dir, "african_languages.png")
)

title: Home
text:
Welcome to my TidyTuesday archive.
Each week, I explore a new dataset through data visualization, statistical analysis, and storytelling using R, ggplot2 and the tidyverse. These projects span topics including sports analytics, geography, text analysis, public health, and environmental data.
Click on any card below to explore the full analysis and code.

title: About This Site
text:
Welcome to my TidyTuesday Gallery!
Each week, I explore a new dataset from the TidyTuesday project, creating visualizations and summaries using R and tidyverse tools. This site serves as a personal record of my weekly explorations, as well as a way to share the insights and graphics I produce with others.
All datasets are publicly available via the TidyTuesday GitHub repository, and this project is entirely open source. You can find the original TidyTuesday datasets here:
This site itself is hosted on GitHub Pages, and the full source code for the website is available here:

title: About This Site
text:
How to Use This Site

Visit the Home page to see a gallery of weekly posts

Click any post to explore individual visualizations and summaries


I hope this site inspires others to explore datasets, create visualizations, and share their own insights with the community. Happy tidying!
